task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app interface being launched or viewed.

task_completion.description: Check interactions between Abiraterone and the specified opioids
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the app displaying interaction search results between Abiraterone and Codeine, Tramadol, or Morphine.
A text editor is open showing the creation or editing of a file named 'abiraterone_pain_audit.txt'.

task_completion.description: Explain the metabolic mechanism causing Codeine treatment failure
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explains that Codeine is a prodrug or requires CYP2D6 for bioactivation, which is inhibited by Abiraterone.

task_completion.description: Explain the suitability of Tramadol as an alternative
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text states that Tramadol is unsuitable because it also requires CYP2D6 for activation and would suffer the same reduced efficacy.

task_completion.description: Recommend the safest opioid based on findings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explicitly recommends Morphine as the safest alternative because its metabolism is not affected by CYP2D6 inhibition.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating and searching within the iChart app, not just relying on internal knowledge or a web browser.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI text editor to write the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI text editor being used to draft the report, rather than terminal commands like 'echo'.

integrity.description: Agent wrote an authentic report rather than copying the prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains a coherent medical explanation in the agent's own words, not just a copy-paste of the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Abiraterone is a moderate CYP2D6 inhibitor. Codeine and Tramadol are prodrugs requiring CYP2D6-mediated bioactivation to their active metabolites for pain relief. Abiraterone impairs this activation, reducing their efficacy. Morphine is not a CYP2D6 substrate (it undergoes glucuronidation) and is unaffected by Abiraterone, making it the correct recommendation. The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app contains this interaction data.
